header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Talent & Organization Development professional who thrives on running cross-functional programs, driving data-driven process improvements, and elevating talent experience. Always seeking ways to improve people experience through human-centered, strategic approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate CEO/HR discovery and {cross|stakeholder} interviews to diagnose absenteeism drivers; analyze leave data to quantify costs
bullets.original: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
bullets.reason: Pair B opener — emphasizes stakeholder interviews + diagnostic work, matches C1's 'consolidate feedback to inform improvements' and 'jump into problems'

bullets.text: Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in {data|leave costs}, leading to {prog|policy redesigns} and first aid training

bullets.reason: Pair A closer — $362K data analysis + executive presentation surfaces data-analytics thread that summary now promises

bullets.text: Consult with executive leadership to redesign {prog|accountability structures} and clarify {cross|cross-functional roles} in support of a 2-year strategic plan

bullets.text: Coordinate 10+ vendors and manage 20+ programs, using feedback loops to refine communication strategy and vendor performance

bullets.reason: Combined former #1 (lifecycles + feedback loops) with former #4 (vendors + multi-calendar) — surfaces the coordinator-specific skills without redundancy

bullets.reason: Reframe to emphasize 'streamlining operations' and 'onboarding' — direct match to C1's onboarding logistics and process improvement responsibilities

bullets.text: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing {prog|program engagement} and driving a 78% program enrollment rate

changeLog: Summary rewritten: emphasizes coordinating multi-stakeholder programs, feedback→process improvement, candidate/employee experience (matches C1's 'partner with recruiters and hiring managers to create candidate experience' + 'identify process improvements')
Keyword groups relabeled for coordinator/ops emphasis (Coordination/Logistics, Candidate/Employee Experience, Programs/Operations)
ODN Oregon: swapped to Pair B (Facilitate/Redesign) — stakeholder interviews + engagement/comms plan map to 'consolidate feedback' and 'school communications' framing
ODN Oregon: corrected company name to 'Organization Development Network (ODN) Oregon' per memory rule
InGenius: title set to 'Program Manager, Enablement' (PM/Engagement role type)
InGenius: Set B bullets — end-to-end lifecycles, cross-functional processes, listening sessions, coordination + multi-calendar scheduling
Vestas: reordered to lead with onboarding (C1 calls out onboarding logistics)
Kronos: reframed 'personalized communication' as 'candidate communication' (C1's candidate experience framing)
Skills: rewrote tech line to feature Asana/Google Workspace/Slack/Zoom — direct match to C1's tooling. Dropped LMS/Canvas/InDesign (not relevant to recruiting coordinator role)
Location: Portland, OR header preserved — Portland local is a real advantage for this 4-day in-office role
